name: Vegetable Pad Thai
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, cabbage, beans) - chopped
1 tbsp tamarind paste (or 1 tsp lemon juice + 1 tsp jaggery)
Spring onion and lime for garnish
substitutions: No rice noodles? Use any flat noodles or even broken spaghetti
No tamarind paste? Use lemon juice + a pinch of jaggery/sugar
No tofu? Use paneer cubes
steps: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 10 mins until soft, then drain
Heat oil in pan, saute garlic till fragrant
Add tofu cubes, fry until light golden
Add vegetables, stir-fry on high flame for 2-3 mins
Add soaked noodles, soy sauce, tamarind paste, and chili flakes
Toss everything together for 3-4 mins until noodles are evenly coated
Garnish with crushed peanuts, spring onion, and a lime wedge
tips: Keep flame high while stir-frying to avoid soggy noodles - this is the biggest mistake beginners make.

name: Tom Yum Soup (Vegetarian)
ingredients: 1 tbsp chili paste (or red chili flakes)
substitutions: No lemongrass? Use extra ginger + lemon zest
No chili paste? Use dry red chilies + a bit of tomato ketchup
steps: Boil stock/water with ginger and garlic for 5 mins
Add mushrooms and tomato, cook for 3-4 mins
Add chili paste and soy sauce, stir well
Turn off heat, add lemon juice (adding it while boiling makes it bitter)
Garnish with coriander and serve hot
tips: Always add lemon juice at the end after removing from heat to keep the soup's flavor bright.

name: Thai Green Curry with Vegetables
ingredients: 2 tbsp green curry paste (or blend green chili + coriander + garlic + ginger)
1 cup mixed vegetables (beans, carrot, bell pepper, baby corn)
substitutions: No coconut milk? Use cashew paste + milk as a substitute
No ready green curry paste? Blend green chilies, coriander stems, garlic, ginger, and a little lemongrass/lemon zest
steps: Heat oil, saute curry paste for 2 mins until fragrant
Add half the coconut milk, cook until oil separates slightly
Add vegetables and tofu/paneer, cook for 5 mins
Add remaining coconut milk, soy sauce, and sugar
Simmer for 8-10 mins until vegetables are tender
Garnish with basil/coriander and serve with rice
tips: Cooking the curry paste in oil first (not directly in liquid) brings out much stronger flavor.

name: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapow Gai)
substitutions: No oyster sauce? Skip it, add 1/2 tsp extra soy sauce
No basil? Use mint + coriander mix as closest substitute
Chicken can be replaced with soy chunks/paneer for veg version
steps: Heat oil, fry garlic and chilies until fragrant
Add minced chicken, cook on high flame until browned
Add soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sugar, mix well
Cook until chicken is fully done, about 5-6 mins
Turn off flame, toss in basil leaves till wilted
Serve over rice, topped with a fried egg
tips: Keep flame high throughout - this dish is meant to be cooked fast and hot, not simmered.

name: Massaman Curry with Chicken and Potatoes
ingredients: 2 tbsp massaman curry paste (or blend dry red chili + cumin + cinnamon + cardamom + garlic)
substitutions: No massaman paste? Use a mix of garam masala + a few dry red chilies + cinnamon as a rough substitute
No tamarind? Use lemon juice
steps: Heat a little coconut milk (thick part) and fry curry paste for 2 mins
Add chicken, cook until it changes color
Add potatoes, onion, and remaining coconut milk
Add tamarind paste and sugar, mix well
Cover and simmer for 20 mins until chicken and potatoes are tender
Garnish with crushed peanuts before serving
tips: This curry tastes even better the next day - great for hostel meal prep.

name: Thai Fried Rice (Khao Pad)
substitutions: No day-old rice? Cook fresh rice and spread it out to cool/dry for 10 mins before frying
Egg can be skipped for vegan version
steps: Heat oil, saute garlic until golden
Push to a side, crack egg in pan and scramble
Add vegetables, stir-fry for 2 mins
Add rice and soy sauce, mix well breaking any lumps
Stir-fry on high heat for 3-4 mins until slightly crisp
Garnish with spring onion and a squeeze of lime
tips: Day-old refrigerated rice fries much better than fresh rice - fresh rice turns mushy.

name: Black Glutinous Rice Pudding with Coconut
substitutions: No black glutinous rice? Use regular black rice or even broken rice with longer cook time
No coconut milk? Use regular milk with 1 tsp desiccated coconut
steps: Drain soaked rice, boil in 2 cups water until soft (25-30 mins)
Add sugar/jaggery and a pinch of salt, mix well
In a separate small pan, warm the coconut milk (do not boil)
Serve rice pudding warm in a bowl, topped with warm coconut milk
tips: This is a great make-ahead dessert - stores well in the fridge for 2 days.

name: Thai Papaya Salad (Som Tam)
equipment: Bowl / Mortar-pestle (or just a bowl and spoon)
ingredients: 1 cup raw papaya, grated (or grated raw mango/cucumber as substitute)
substitutions: No raw papaya? Grated raw mango or cucumber works as a fresh substitute
Traditional fish sauce replaced here with soy sauce for veg version
steps: In a bowl, mix lemon juice, soy sauce, sugar, and chilies to make dressing
Add grated papaya and tomato, toss well
Let it sit for 5 mins so flavors combine
Top with crushed peanuts before serving
tips: No cooking required - perfect quick, no-equipment hostel recipe.

name: Veg Hakka Noodles
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (cabbage, carrot, capsicum), julienned
substitutions: No specific vegetables? Use whatever is on hand - onion, beans, peas all work
No chili sauce? Use red chili flakes
steps: Boil noodles until just cooked (al dente), drain and toss with a little oil to prevent sticking
Heat oil in pan, saute garlic for 30 secs
Add vegetables, stir-fry on high flame for 3-4 mins
Add noodles, soy sauce, vinegar, and chili sauce
Toss everything on high flame for 2-3 mins
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot
tips: Undercook the noodles slightly before stir-frying, since they'll cook further in the pan.

name: Chilli Paneer
substitutions: No cornflour? Use rice flour or all-purpose flour
Paneer can be replaced with tofu or chicken for other variants
steps: Coat paneer cubes with cornflour and shallow fry until golden, set aside
In the same pan, saute garlic and ginger till fragrant
Add onion and capsicum, stir-fry on high for 2 mins (keep slightly crunchy)
Add soy sauce, chili sauce, and vinegar
Add fried paneer back in, toss to coat evenly
Cook for 2 more mins and serve hot
tips: Don't overcook the capsicum/onion - restaurant-style chilli paneer keeps them crunchy, not soft.

name: Egg Fried Rice
substitutions: No day-old rice? Spread freshly cooked rice on a plate to cool and dry for 10-15 mins first
steps: Heat oil, saute garlic
Scramble eggs in the pan, set aside a portion for garnish
Add vegetables, stir fry for 2 mins
Add rice and soy sauce, mix well
Add scrambled egg back in, toss on high heat for 2-3 mins
Garnish with spring onion
tips: Great way to use leftover rice - a hostel staple that takes under 15 minutes.

name: Chicken Manchurian
ingredients: 1 tsp cornflour + 1/4 cup water (slurry for gravy)
substitutions: No egg? Marinate chicken with just cornflour and a little water instead
For dry version, skip the cornflour slurry step
steps: Marinate chicken with cornflour, egg, salt, and pepper for 10 mins
Shallow fry chicken pieces until golden and cooked through, set aside
In same pan, saute garlic, ginger, and onion till fragrant
Add soy sauce, chili sauce, and ketchup
Add a little water and the cornflour slurry, stir until it thickens into a light gravy
Add fried chicken back in, toss to coat well
Garnish with spring onion and serve
tips: For 'dry' Manchurian (party-starter style), skip the slurry and just toss chicken in the sauce directly.

name: Vegetable Manchow Soup
ingredients: 1/2 cup finely chopped mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, cabbage)
substitutions: No fried noodle topping available? Skip it, soup tastes fine without
steps: Heat oil, saute garlic until fragrant
Add vegetables, stir fry for 2 mins
Add stock/water, soy sauce, and chili sauce, bring to a boil
Add cornflour slurry slowly while stirring, until soup thickens slightly
Simmer for 5 mins and serve hot, topped with crispy noodles if available
tips: Chop vegetables very fine - this soup is meant to have small bits, not chunky pieces.

name: Schezwan Fried Rice
ingredients: 2 tbsp schezwan sauce (or 2 tbsp chili sauce + extra garlic for a rough substitute)
substitutions: No schezwan sauce? Blend dry red chilies + garlic + a little vinegar and soy sauce as a substitute
steps: Heat oil, saute garlic until golden
Add vegetables, stir-fry for 2-3 mins on high flame
Add rice, schezwan sauce, and soy sauce
Toss everything well on high heat for 3-4 mins
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot
tips: This is spicier than regular fried rice - adjust sauce quantity to taste.

name: Chicken Hakka Noodles
substitutions: Chicken can be replaced with egg or extra vegetables for other diet types
steps: Boil noodles until al dente, drain and set aside with a little oil tossed in
Heat oil, cook chicken slices until done, set aside
In same pan, saute garlic, add vegetables, stir-fry for 2-3 mins
Add noodles, chicken, soy sauce, vinegar, and chili sauce
Toss everything on high flame for 2-3 mins and serve
tips: Cut chicken thin and uniform so it cooks fast and evenly on high heat.

name: Veg Spring Rolls (Pan-fried, no deep fry)
ingredients: 6-8 spring roll wrappers (or thin chapati/roti cut into rectangles as substitute)
substitutions: No spring roll wrappers available in hostel? Use thin roti/chapati strips as wrap
steps: Heat oil, saute garlic, add shredded vegetables and soy sauce, stir-fry for 3 mins, let cool
Place filling on wrapper, roll tightly, seal edge with cornflour-water paste
Heat a little oil in pan, shallow fry rolls on medium flame until golden on all sides
Serve with extra chili sauce or ketchup
tips: Pan-frying instead of deep-frying works well in hostel rooms without needing a fryer.

name: Teriyaki Chicken
substitutions: No rice vinegar? Use regular vinegar or lemon juice
Honey can replace sugar for a glossier glaze
steps: Mix soy sauce, sugar, vinegar, and garlic to make teriyaki sauce
Heat oil in pan, cook chicken on medium flame until browned on both sides and cooked through
Pour teriyaki sauce over chicken, let it simmer and thicken for 3-4 mins, coating the chicken
Garnish with sesame seeds, serve with rice
tips: Let the sauce reduce and get sticky/glossy on the chicken - don't rush this step for the authentic glaze.

name: Vegetable Fried Rice (Yaki Meshi style)
substitutions: No sesame oil? Regular cooking oil works, flavor will be slightly different but fine
steps: Heat oil, scramble egg, set aside
Add vegetables, stir-fry for 2-3 mins
Add rice and soy sauce, mix well
Add egg back in, toss on high heat for 2 mins
Garnish with spring onion
tips: A light hand with soy sauce keeps this dish from tasting too similar to Chinese fried rice - it should taste more delicate.

name: Simple Miso-style Ramen (Instant Upgrade)
ingredients: 1 pack instant noodles (discard flavor packet or use half)
1 tbsp miso paste (or 1 tbsp soy sauce + 1 tsp peanut butter as substitute)
substitutions: No miso paste? Mix 1 tbsp soy sauce with 1 tsp peanut butter or tahini for a similar umami-rich base
steps: Boil water, add noodles and vegetables, cook for 3 mins
Lower heat, stir in miso paste (or substitute) until dissolved
Add a tsp of oil for richness
Top with sliced boiled egg and serve hot
tips: This is a great budget upgrade to instant noodles using items already in most hostel rooms.

name: Onigiri (Rice Balls)
ingredients: 2 tbsp filling of choice (e.g. sauteed vegetables, paneer bhurji, or pickle)
substitutions: No nori? Skip it, onigiri still works without the wrap
Filling can be anything savory - even leftover sabzi works
steps: Wet hands slightly and rub a pinch of salt on palms
Take a handful of warm rice, flatten slightly and place filling in the center
Shape into a triangle or ball, pressing firmly so it holds together
Wrap a strip of nori around the base if available
Repeat with remaining rice
tips: No equipment needed at all - perfect for hostel rooms with no stove access, using just leftover rice.

name: Japanese Curry with Vegetables
ingredients: 1 tbsp curry powder (or garam masala + turmeric as substitute)
substitutions: No Japanese curry powder? Mix garam masala, turmeric, and a pinch of cumin
steps: Heat oil, saute onion until soft
Add potato and carrot, cook for 3-4 mins
Add flour and curry powder, mix to coat vegetables
Add water gradually, stirring to avoid lumps
Simmer covered for 15-18 mins until vegetables are soft and gravy thickens
Add soy sauce, mix, and serve with rice
tips: The flour is what gives Japanese curry its thick, stew-like texture compared to Indian curries.

name: Kimchi-style Fried Rice (Quick Version)
substitutions: No real kimchi available? This quick pickled-cabbage-flavor version mimics the taste using pantry staples
Gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) can be substituted with regular red chili flakes + a pinch of paprika
steps: Toss chopped cabbage with vinegar, sugar, and chili flakes, let sit 10 mins
Heat oil in pan, add the quick-pickled cabbage, stir fry for 2 mins
Add rice and soy sauce, mix well and fry for 3-4 mins
Fry an egg separately, sunny-side up
Top rice with the fried egg and serve
tips: For a closer authentic taste, use real kimchi if available at any Korean/Asian grocery store instead of the quick substitute.

name: Bibimbap-style Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 tbsp chili paste (gochujang substitute: mix chili flakes + soy sauce + a little sugar)
substitutions: No gochujang? Mix 1 tbsp chili sauce/ketchup + 1 tsp soy sauce + pinch of sugar as a substitute
steps: Saute each vegetable separately with a little oil and salt, keep them separate
Place warm rice in a bowl
Arrange each sauteed vegetable in sections around the rice
Top with fried egg in the center
Add a spoon of chili paste substitute on top, drizzle sesame oil, mix everything before eating
tips: Keeping vegetables separate while cooking (not mixed) is what gives bibimbap its signature look and layered flavor.

name: Korean-style Spicy Chicken Stir-fry
ingredients: 1 tbsp chili paste (gochujang substitute: chili flakes + ketchup + soy sauce)
Spring onion and sesame seeds for garnish
substitutions: No gochujang? Mix 1 tbsp ketchup + 1 tsp chili flakes + 1 tsp soy sauce as substitute
steps: Mix chili paste substitute, soy sauce, sugar, and garlic in a bowl - this is the marinade/sauce
Heat oil, cook chicken on medium-high until browned and nearly cooked, about 5-6 mins
Pour sauce mix over chicken, toss to coat evenly
Cook for 3-4 more mins until sauce thickens and coats the chicken
Garnish with spring onion and sesame seeds, serve with rice
tips: This dish is spicy by default - reduce chili paste quantity for a milder version.

name: Korean Cucumber Salad (Oi Muchim)
substitutions: No sesame oil? Regular oil with a pinch of roasted sesame seeds if available
steps: Sprinkle salt on cucumber slices, let sit for 5 mins, then squeeze out excess water
Mix chili flakes, vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, and sugar in a bowl
Toss cucumber slices in the dressing
Serve immediately as a side dish
tips: No cooking needed - great quick side dish for hostel rooms without a stove.

name: Korean Ramen Upgrade (Budae-jjigae style)
ingredients: 2 sausages or leftover cooked chicken, sliced
1 tbsp chili paste (ketchup + chili flakes substitute)
substitutions: No sausages? Use any leftover cooked meat or paneer for veg version
steps: Boil water in a pot, add chili paste and soy sauce, mix well
Add sausages/chicken and cabbage, boil for 5 mins
Add noodles and its seasoning packet (half quantity), cook for 3 mins
Crack an egg into the pot, let it poach for 1-2 mins
Serve hot directly from the pot
tips: This 'army stew' style dish was originally invented from combining random available ingredients - perfect philosophy for hostel cooking.

name: Vietnamese Pho-style Noodle Soup (Quick Veg Version)
ingredients: Lime, coriander, bean sprouts for garnish
substitutions: No star anise? Skip it, use a pinch of cinnamon powder for a similar warm note
steps: Boil rice noodles separately according to pack instructions, drain
In another pot, boil stock/water with ginger and star anise for 5 mins
Add mushrooms/spinach and soy sauce, simmer for 5 mins
Pour hot broth over noodles in a bowl
Garnish with lime, coriander, and bean sprouts if available
tips: Simmering the broth with ginger and spices even for just 10 minutes makes a big difference vs. plain water.

name: Vietnamese Spring Rolls (Fresh, No-Cook)
ingredients: 2 tbsp peanut butter + 1 tbsp soy sauce + water (dipping sauce)
substitutions: No rice paper? Use thin lettuce leaves as a wrap instead
No peanut butter for sauce? Use a mix of ketchup and chili sauce
steps: Dip rice paper sheet in warm water for 5-10 secs until soft
Lay it flat, place vegetables and herbs in the center
Fold sides in and roll tightly like a wrap
Repeat with remaining sheets
Mix peanut butter, soy sauce, and a little water for dipping sauce
Serve fresh rolls with the dipping sauce
tips: Zero cooking required - ideal for hostel rooms with no cooking equipment at all.

name: Vietnamese Lemongrass Chicken Stir-fry
ingredients: 1 stalk lemongrass, finely chopped (or 1 tsp lemon zest as substitute)
substitutions: No lemongrass available? Use lemon zest + a small piece of ginger for a similar citrusy-fresh note
steps: Marinate chicken with lemongrass/lemon zest, garlic, soy sauce, and sugar for 10 mins
Heat oil in pan, cook marinated chicken on medium-high heat until browned and cooked through
Garnish with chili and coriander
Serve with rice
tips: Marinating even for just 10 minutes makes a noticeable difference in flavor depth.

name: Vietnamese Stir-fried Noodles with Vegetables
ingredients: Crushed peanuts and lime for garnish
substitutions: Any noodles on hand work fine - this dish is flexible with noodle type
steps: Soak/boil noodles according to package instructions, drain
Heat oil, saute garlic until fragrant
Add vegetables, stir-fry for 3 mins
Add noodles, soy sauce, and sugar, toss well on high heat for 3-4 mins
Garnish with crushed peanuts and a squeeze of lime
tips: Fresh lime juice at the end brightens up the whole dish - don't skip it.
